title: Together AI brings Thinking Machines Lab's new model Inkling on day 0
gist: Thinking Machines Lab released Inkling, a 975B-parameter (40B active) multimodal mixture-of-experts model with a 1M-token context window, controllable reasoning effort, and a novel attention/convolution architecture, and Together AI is serving it day-0 on its Serverless platform with a custom FlashAttention-4-based kernel and OpenAI-compatible API.

insights.claim: Inkling is a decoder-only mixture-of-experts model with 975 billion total parameters and 40 billion active parameters per token, and a 1 million token context window, released by Thinking Machines Lab.
insights.speaker: Jue Wang, Wei Gong, Yineng Zhang, Hiral Jasani
insights.so_what: A 975B-parameter frontier-scale MoE release from Thinking Machines Lab is a significant new entrant at the top end of the model-capability race, directly relevant to competitive positioning versus OpenAI, Anthropic, Google DeepMind, and Meta's largest models.
insights.quote: Inkling is a decoder-only mixture-of-experts model with 975B total parameters, 40B active parameters per token, and a context window of 1M tokens.
insights.locator: A new architecture for reasoning and multimodality

insights.claim: Instead of RoPE or absolute position embeddings, Inkling uses a learned, query-conditioned relative attention bias that combines the standard query-key similarity score with an additional score based on relative token distance.
insights.speaker: Jue Wang, Wei Gong, Yineng Zhang, Hiral Jasani
insights.so_what: A departure from RoPE (the current industry-standard position encoding) signals Thinking Machines Lab is pursuing architectural differentiation rather than following the consensus Transformer recipe, which is a signal of technical ambition worth tracking for future model releases.
insights.quote: Rather than using RoPE or absolute position embeddings, Inkling incorporates token position directly into attention through a learned, query-conditioned relative bias.
insights.locator: A new architecture for reasoning and multimodality

insights.claim: Inkling mixes five local sliding-window attention layers with one full-causal-attention layer in a repeating pattern, and adds 'sconv' -- a lightweight channelwise causal convolution with a four-token receptive field -- applied to key/value streams and to attention/feed-forward outputs, to combine adjacent-token information cheaply.
insights.speaker: Jue Wang, Wei Gong, Yineng Zhang, Hiral Jasani
insights.so_what: This local/global attention plus convolution hybrid is an efficiency-oriented design choice aimed at controlling inference cost at long context lengths, relevant to assessing Inkling's serving economics versus dense-attention competitors.
insights.quote: The standard architecture uses five local-attention layers followed by one full-attention layer, allowing most layers to focus efficiently on recent context while periodically integrating information across the full sequence.
insights.locator: A new architecture for reasoning and multimodality

insights.claim: Inkling's mixture-of-experts feed-forward layers use a 'shared expert sink' design that normalizes shared experts and selected routed experts together in the same weighting computation, letting the shared path dynamically compete for mixture weight on each token, unlike conventional shared-expert MoE designs.
insights.speaker: Jue Wang, Wei Gong, Yineng Zhang, Hiral Jasani
insights.so_what: A novel MoE routing mechanism is a potential source of quality/efficiency advantage; worth monitoring whether this becomes an architecture other labs adopt.
insights.quote: Unlike conventional shared-expert MoE designs, Inkling normalizes the shared experts and selected routed experts together, allowing the shared path to dynamically compete for mixture weight on each token.
insights.locator: A new architecture for reasoning and multimodality

insights.claim: Inkling natively accepts text, image, and audio inputs through lightweight embedding towers that convert image patches and quantized audio features into embeddings inserted directly into the same decoder input sequence as text tokens, producing text-only outputs.
insights.speaker: Jue Wang, Wei Gong, Yineng Zhang, Hiral Jasani
insights.so_what: A single unified decoder handling three modalities without separate pipelines competes directly with Gemini's and GPT-4o's natively multimodal approach, positioning Inkling as a frontier-class multimodal alternative.
insights.quote: Lightweight embedding towers transform image patches and quantized audio features into embeddings with the same width as text-token embeddings.
insights.locator: Unified text, image, and audio inputs

insights.claim: Inkling supports a controllable 'inference effort' / reasoning-effort parameter that lets developers trade off reasoning depth, token usage, and latency per request, and is post-trained across scientific reasoning, coding, agentic workflows, forecasting, and calibrated prediction tasks.
insights.speaker: Jue Wang, Wei Gong, Yineng Zhang, Hiral Jasani
insights.so_what: Adjustable-effort reasoning at the API level (matching the pattern set by OpenAI's o-series and Anthropic's extended thinking) suggests this control mechanism is becoming a table-stakes feature across frontier labs, useful for tracking API design convergence.
insights.quote: It supports controllable inference effort, allowing developers to adjust how much reasoning the model applies based on the needs of each task.

insights.claim: Inkling is explicitly post-trained on forecasting and calibrated-prediction tasks, extending its intended use beyond conventional question-answering into applications requiring well-calibrated uncertainty estimates.
insights.speaker: Jue Wang, Wei Gong, Yineng Zhang, Hiral Jasani
insights.so_what: Calibrated forecasting capability targets decision-support and prediction-market-adjacent use cases, a differentiated positioning versus generalist chat-tuned models.
insights.quote: Inkling is also post-trained on forecasting and calibrated prediction tasks. This expands the model's usefulness beyond conventional question answering to applications where representing uncertainty and producing well-calibrated estimates are important.
insights.locator: Strong performance on challenging reasoning tasks

insights.claim: Together AI serves Inkling on day 0 of its release via Together AI Serverless with no capacity wait, no infrastructure provisioning, and a single API call handling all three input modalities (text, image, audio) plus a 1M context window and OpenAI-compatible API.
insights.speaker: Jue Wang, Wei Gong, Yineng Zhang, Hiral Jasani
insights.so_what: Landing day-0 hosting rights for a frontier lab's flagship model release is a competitive distribution win for Together AI over rival inference platforms (e.g., Fireworks, Baseten, Groq, hyperscaler model gardens), and signals Together AI's standing relationship with emerging frontier labs beyond just open-source model serving.
insights.quote: Day 0 access, zero setup: Inkling is live on Together AI Serverless today. No waiting for capacity, no infrastructure to provision, no GPUs to manage.

insights.claim: Together AI built an optimized FlashAttention-4-based attention kernel specifically to efficiently support Inkling's non-standard query-conditioned relative attention mechanism in production serving.
insights.speaker: Jue Wang, Wei Gong, Yineng Zhang, Hiral Jasani
insights.so_what: Demonstrates Together AI's inference-stack engineering capability to rapidly build custom kernels for novel model architectures, which is a technical moat versus generic inference providers that can only serve standard Transformer architectures efficiently.
insights.quote: On Together AI, Inkling runs with an optimized FlashAttention-4–based attention kernel designed to efficiently support its query-conditioned relative attention mechanism in production.

insights.claim: Together AI positions its unified multimodal API as removing the tradeoff between latency, speed, and operational stability that would otherwise come from stitching together separate preprocessing pipelines for text, image, and audio.
insights.speaker: Jue Wang, Wei Gong, Yineng Zhang, Hiral Jasani
insights.so_what: Reinforces Together AI's platform pitch of being a one-stop multimodal inference layer, relevant when comparing it to competitors requiring multi-vendor pipelines for multimodal apps.
insights.quote: Together’s unified solution removes the tradeoff between latency, speed and operational stability.

insights.claim: The published code sample for calling Inkling on Together AI shows the reasoning_effort API parameter still marked as a placeholder ('[SUPPORTED VALUE]'), indicating the controllable-reasoning feature's exact API surface was not finalized at the time of the day-0 launch post.
insights.speaker: Jue Wang, Wei Gong, Yineng Zhang, Hiral Jasani
insights.so_what: A minor but concrete signal that the integration was shipped in a rush to hit day-0, suggesting some API details may still change shortly after this announcement.
insights.quote:
# Replace with the final supported API parameter.
reasoning_effort="[SUPPORTED VALUE]",

source_meta.author: Jue Wang, Wei Gong, Yineng Zhang, Hiral Jasani
